title: verify-runtime-analytics-gui was not idempotent: leftover security store on re-run hit login form
description: verify-runtime-analytics-gui.mjs points LOCALAPPDATA at persisted .fixtures-observability/<state> dirs and relies on signInFirstRun (first-run FirstRunSetup). The first run provisions a guiverifier Super User into <state>/SpecterStudio/security; a re-run WITHOUT a fresh seed then shows the login form instead, so signInFirstRun's page.fill(#awkit-setup-display) times out at 30s and the walkthrough silently reports 0/4 (exit 1). Passed 36/36 only right after seed-observability-fixtures --fresh. The other four sweep verifiers avoid this by using auto-cleaned temp profiles. FIX (branch fix/gui-verifier-secure-login-gate): walkState now rmSync's <state>/SpecterStudio/security before each launch so every run is a clean first-run; observability fixture untouched. Proven idempotent: 36/36 twice in a row with no re-seed.
close_reason: Fixed on branch fix/gui-verifier-secure-login-gate: walkState clears <state>/SpecterStudio/security before each launch; proven idempotent 36/36 twice with no re-seed.

title: Oracle Drivers GUI verifier: thread SecurityGate first-run auth + seed validation store into an isolated profile
description: verify-oracle-drivers-gui.mjs is the one GUI verifier bd awkit-gmn names that the general splash/gate shared-harness fix does NOT resolve on its own. It already has resolveMainWindow (splash half) but (1) needs signInFirstRun to pass the SecurityGate (PR #15), AND (2) asserts a real Java runtime 'Local-JDK-17' + ojdbc bundle 'Oracle-ojdbc17-local-19c-validation' that live in the developer's REAL profile store and drives java.testBridge + drivers.testLoad through real IPC (needs build:oracle-bridge + a real java.exe + ojdbc jar). To make it self-contained + non-destructive like the other GUI verifiers, isolate LOCALAPPDATA to a temp profile, seed the Java runtime + driver bundle into it (read the paths from the real validation store or re-run add-java-runtime.mts/import-driver-bundle.mts against the temp dir), then signInFirstRun, then run. Belongs with the Oracle epic's GUI validation gate. Not attempted in the 2026-07-19 general verifier sweep (would need the Oracle validation environment).

title: Flow Designer GUI verifier: modernize stale inspector-panel geometry assertions (post-Hologram floating drawer)
description: verify-flow-designer-gui.mjs now launches + signs in past the SecurityGate (bd awkit-gmn fix) and 19/24 pass. The 5 remaining failures are STALE geometry assertions from before the Hologram floating-drawer re-skin, NOT the splash/gate bug and NOT a product regression. Current design (global.css ~8286-8358): .designer-right-drawer-slot is position:absolute (floating overlay) and the canvas inset is done via padding-right on .flow-designer-body; .react-flow-shell legitimately stays full-width. The verifier still asserts the OLD docked-column invariant canvasEngineRight <= panelLeft (fails ~434px) plus pre-DPR thresholds (collapsed rail <=64 vs actual ~83 CSS px at DPR 1.2; panelRight/panelTop off by 1-3px sub-pixel). Fix: update readInspectorGeometry + the 5 checks to assert the floating-drawer invariants (panel contained within .designer-canvas with ~2px epsilon; usable canvas body inset via padding-right so content doesn't sit under the panel; collapsed rail returns canvas width) instead of engine-column shrink. Needs design-intent confirmation on the collapsed-rail target width.

No P0/P1 found; confirmed pre-existing GUI-verifier bug (awkit-gmn) reproduces on verify:reports; found doc drift (CURRENT_STATE.md/HANDOFF.md describe merged PR #14/#15 as uncommitted) - flagged, not fixed.

title: Revoke other sessions on password change (session rotation)
description: Finding from awkit-ekd review: AuthenticationService.changePassword does not revoke the user's other active sessions. Add revoke-all-except-current after a successful change. Add a verify:auth case.
close_reason: Session rotation implemented: changePassword revokes all other sessions except current (revokeOthersForUser → revokeSessionsForUserExcept). verify:auth 45/45 incl 4 new checks; build clean.

title: SecurityStore: cross-process single-writer lock (DurableLockStore) + single-instance app lock
description: Finding from awkit-ekd review: SecurityStore has no cross-process mutex and app sets no requestSingleInstanceLock; two AWKIT processes can lose writes to security.sqlite. Reuse src/runner/store/DurableLockStore around SecurityStore writes (as SqliteRuntimeStore does) and/or add app.requestSingleInstanceLock in app/main/main.ts. Plan §17.
close_reason: Added the Electron single-instance lock in app/main/main.ts (app.requestSingleInstanceLock; second launch focuses the running window via second-instance and quits before opening any window/store). Prevents two SpecterStudio processes from racing on security.sqlite / ui-settings per profile — the stated risk. New verify:single-instance 3/3; settings-persistence 3/3 + capacity-settings 12/12 confirm no regression. DurableLockStore-around-writes remains an optional defense-in-depth (not needed given the process-level guard).

title: branding-splash broke firstWindow()-based Electron GUI verifiers
description: The branding-splash feature (PR #12) shows a splash BrowserWindow first, so Playwright's app.firstWindow() now returns the splash — which has no window.playwrightFlowStudio preload bridge. Every GUI verifier that does app.firstWindow() then win.evaluate(() => window.playwrightFlowStudio...) throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading settings)'. Confirmed on verify:settings-persistence (fixed in the oracle epic via a resolveMainWindow helper that polls app.windows() for the bridge). Likely also affects verify:capacity-settings-gui, verify:instance-monitor-gui, verify:runtime-analytics-gui, verify:workflow-builder, verify:flow-designer, verify:reports (all use _electron + firstWindow). Fix: add a shared resolveMainWindow(app) helper (see scripts/verify-oracle-drivers-gui.mjs / verify-settings-persistence.mjs) that waits for the window exposing window.playwrightFlowStudio.settings, and use it instead of firstWindow(). NOT an Oracle-refactor defect.
notes: EXPANDED ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-18 audit): the splash is only HALF the breakage. PR #15 (Secure Login) added SecurityGate to main.tsx, which mounts ONLY sign-in surfaces until auth — the real <App/> shell (and all nav-items/routes) never mounts pre-auth. So every app-shell GUI verifier needs BOTH fixes: (1) resolveMainWindow(app) polling app.windows() for window.playwrightFlowStudio.settings (splash has no bridge), AND (2) isolate LOCALAPPDATA to a fresh temp dir + drive first-run (provision Super User -> auto sign-in) to reach the shell. Reference implementation now COMPLETE + verified in scripts/verify-reports-gui.mjs (31/31). Note verify-oracle-drivers-gui/verify-settings-persistence were measured 30/30 3/3 on the pre-secure-login Oracle branch; they likely regressed on merged main and need the auth-threading half too. Remaining to fix with the same recipe: verify-flow-designer-gui, verify-workflow-builder-gui, verify-instance-monitor-gui (already isolates+seeds; add auth), verify-capacity-settings-gui, verify-runtime-analytics-gui (already seeds observability fixtures; add auth). Each needs individual re-verification (some have data-dependent assertions that may need fixture seeding, unlike reports which accepts empty states).
close_reason: GUI-verifier sweep landed in PR #16: shared gui-verify-harness.mjs fixes splash+SecurityGate across capacity-settings/instance-monitor/runtime-analytics/workflow-builder/flow-designer. Residuals split to awkit-9p6 (flow-designer geometry) + awkit-xjv (oracle-drivers-gui). Idempotency defect awkit-7ek found+fixed. All counts re-verified 2026-07-19.

comments.author: MohammadAbwini98

Residuals split out: flow-designer's 5 remaining checks are stale post-Hologram geometry assertions -> awkit-9p6; verify-oracle-drivers-gui needs auth + Oracle validation-store seeding -> awkit-xjv. Keeping awkit-gmn open until those two close.
